title: Cooking smoke control during an outage
summary: A boundary for emergency cooking when power is out and people are tempted to use unsafe flames, grills, stoves, or fuel indoors.
content:
A boundary for emergency cooking when power is out and people are tempted to use unsafe flames, grills, stoves, or fuel indoors.

This card improves cooking smoke control during an outage by making the action small, visible, and reversible. It helps a tired group avoid hidden assumptions while preserving emergency-service priority.

Core sequence: 1) Use only cooking methods intended for the location and keep combustion devices outside sleeping air. 2) Choose cold shelf-stable food when ventilation, fuel, or supervision is uncertain. 3) Keep children, loose clothes, paper, bedding, and fuel away from the cooking area. 4) Stop cooking if smoke, dizziness, headache, flame spread, or arguments over placement begin.

Stop point: Do not use cooking smoke control during an outage to bypass emergency responders, utility professionals, medical advice, or evacuation instructions.
materials: cold-meal backup list prepared specifically for Cooking smoke control during an outage
dated owner note for Cooking smoke control during an outage
separate safe place linked to Food · Cooking smoke control
visible redirection mark for Cooking smoke control during an outage
steps: Use only cooking methods intended for the location and keep combustion devices outside sleeping air.
Choose cold shelf-stable food when ventilation, fuel, or supervision is uncertain.
Keep children, loose clothes, paper, bedding, and fuel away from the cooking area.
Stop cooking if smoke, dizziness, headache, flame spread, or arguments over placement begin.
warnings: Do not continue cooking smoke control during an outage if the action increases fire, fumes, contamination, injury, violence, or official-order risk.
Do not leave cooking smoke control during an outage dependent on a single tired person without a written handover.
common_mistakes: Using cooking smoke control during an outage without checking the weakest person first makes the routine look complete while help is still missing.
Changing cooking smoke control during an outage verbally without changing the visible note creates two competing versions.
alternatives: If cooking smoke control during an outage cannot be done as planned, reduce it to a marked safe area, one named owner, and one next review.
If equipment for cooking smoke control during an outage is missing, use observation, distance, labeling, and official help instead of improvised high-risk repair.
failure_signs: The visible note for cooking smoke control during an outage no longer matches what people are actually doing.
Someone says they assumed cooking smoke control during an outage was handled, but no time, owner, or result is written.
when_not_to_use: Do not use cooking smoke control during an outage to bypass emergency responders, utility professionals, medical advice, or evacuation instructions.
Do not continue cooking smoke control during an outage inside smoke, floodwater, sewage, unstable walls, or a location with suspected gas or electrical danger.
short_term: Before moving on, make the first result of cooking smoke control during an outage visible and confirm one person can explain it in under thirty seconds.
long_term: After real use, improve cooking smoke control during an outage by replacing unclear wording, weak storage choices, and unsafe shortcuts with local practice notes.
if_method_fails: If cooking smoke control during an outage fails, mark the unsafe part unavailable, retreat to the last safe option, and request official or qualified support when needed.
environment_notes: Adapt cooking smoke control during an outage to apartment blocks, farms, islands, camps, shops, ferries, heat, smoke, low light, noise, and mobility limits.
